summary: Good‑sounding indoor recording captures a scorching Roanoke performance with US Blues, Scarlet Fire and Crazy Fingers blazing.
review: The tapes from the July 7 1987 Roanoke shows convey the hot, high‑energy vibe that fans remember – Jerry leads the band into fiery renditions of US Blues, Scarlet Fire, Crazy Fingers and Comes A Time, while the new indoor PA adds a slight echo but still sounds clear. Reviewers repeatedly praise the performance and note the recording is solid enough to enjoy without major flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: 13 consistently enthusiastic reviews (mostly 4‑5 stars) highlight strong musical performance and a clear, listenable recording; no significant complaints about audio quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No major audio complaints; reviewers note the venue was small and “echoey” with an indoor PA setup, but the energy comes through clearly.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Two‑night run was hot and tightly paced; the band stayed upbeat, cranking out high‑energy jams that flowed well despite the intimate arena setting.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for leading the charge, delivering snappy vocal slides and strong guitar work.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers received compliments for perfect beat and great drumming throughout.
